description: Synthetic political or moral community label
interpretive_warning: Do not treat synthetic groups as real political populations
Values alone do not explain polarization
Trust is shaped by institutions identity and history

description: Strength of political or group identity salience
interpretive_warning: Identity can shape how evidence is received
Exposure is not identical to belief or persuasion

description: Perceived danger betrayal or moral threat
interpretive_warning: Threat can compress complexity and intensify exclusion

description: Amplified anger disgust contempt or humiliation
interpretive_warning: Outrage can signal injustice or be manipulated

description: Confidence in shared institutions evidence and process
interpretive_warning: Trust can dampen polarization when institutions are legitimate

description: Meaningful contact or recognition across groups
interpretive_warning: Contact must be high-quality to reduce hostility

description: Synthetic risk of excluding opponents from moral concern
interpretive_warning: Not a diagnostic score for real persons
